statement: The DTA AI technical standard mandates controls across Discover/Operate/Retire — including the Operate (runtime) phase — but agent-enterprise explicitly is 'not a runtime sandbox' and disclaims downstream model/agent behaviour, so as scoped it cannot satisfy operate-phase assurance and is at best a partial, pre-deployment control.

evidence.quote: The AI technical standard provides requirements/recommendations across three lifecycle phases — Discover, Operate and Retire... including operate-phase (runtime) controls.
Focus: accountability, mandatory use-case impact assessment, procurement safeguards, transparency, technical assurance.

falsifier: Reframe scope honestly: position agent-enterprise as covering only the Discover/build-and-procure controls and the artefact-supply-chain slice, with an explicit documented hand-off to runtime tooling for the Operate phase — a complementary control, not end-to-end assurance.
